headlines.title: Two U.S. service members killed in Jordan and another is missing, military says
headlines.description: Two U.S. service members were killed Friday while defending against an Iranian ballistic missile and drone attack in Jordan, and another service member is missing, U.S.

headlines.title: Maps: Wildfire smoke forecast for the Midwest and northern U.S.
headlines.description: As heat waves and wildfire smoke sweep the country, see where it could be affecting you on the map and what you can do to curb the health risks.

headlines.title: Trump fumes over Canadian wildfire smoke and issues tariff threat
headlines.description: President Donald Trump on Friday pledged to hold Canada responsible for costs associated with unhealthy air quality in the U.S. due to spreading smoke from the Canadian wildfires.

headlines.title: Idaho pushed anti-trans laws for years. Then a teen died.
headlines.description: The life and death of May Pollard, 16, provides a window into the tragic effect that legislation and political rhetoric have had on trans people in America.

headlines.title: Life and death in the Senate, and a dinosaur’s $50 million payday: The news quiz
headlines.description: Lionel Messi leads La Albiceleste back to the World Cup final, the House tries changing the clock, and health officials look for the cause of the cyclosporiasis outbreak.

headlines.title: How the World Cup final is starting to look a lot like the Super Bowl
headlines.description: For American viewers of the World Cup final on Sunday, the sport the rest of the world knows as football will closely resemble the one Americans call the same name.

headlines.title: Rep. Ralph Norman launches campaign to replace Lindsey Graham in Senate race
headlines.description: Rep. Ralph Norman, R-S.C., announced Saturday that he plans to run in the special Senate primary election to fill the late GOP Sen. Lindsey Graham’s seat.

headlines.title: Rhode Island fugitive captured after more than 20 years on the run
headlines.description: One of Rhode Island’s “longest-wanted” fugitives was finally arrested this week after 21 years on the run, according to the U.S.

headlines.title: Iran and Kuwait report attacks on water and power infrastructure as strikes escalate
headlines.description: Critical water and power infrastructure sites have been damaged as the U.S. and Iran continue to trade strikes, while the parched Middle East reels from temperatures of 100 degrees and beyond.
